what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block item that inventories approximately eight graph layers in the system and states that their nodes already converge on one resolver while their edge vocabulary already unifies through one registry mechanism, leaving only edge-source convergence as an open issue.
observations: Lists eight graph layers: execution/workflow, corpus semantic relations, board typed-edge graph, type graph, navigation, code-dependency, extraction/dragnet semantic, and field-index triple-store.

Cites concrete files such as runtime/relation_types.py, contracts/node_record.py, runtime/scheduler.py, cc_board.py, registry.py, design/_system/navgraph.py, design/_system/codeedges.py, .data/store/extractions/, and .data/store/code_archaeology/field_index.jsonl.
Claims the edge-vocabulary mechanism RelationTypeRegistry is reused verbatim by board_edges/.
Notes nodes already converge on one resolver and edge vocabulary already unifies on one registry.
Flags that edge-SOURCE is not converged and points to F6.
Mentions record counts: 18.8k extraction records and 11.5k field-index triples, with the latter marked UNTRAVERSED.
Uses noticeboard metadata YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
standouts: The central claim that a single node resolver and a single edge-registry mechanism already serve eight heterogeneous graph layers.
The explicit remaining gap is edge-source convergence, not resolver or registry unification.
The field-index triple-store is called out as large (11.5k typed triples) but currently untraversed.
concerns: The layer count is approximate (≈8) and the list mixes architectural concerns at different granularities.
The file asserts convergence but gives no evidence or mechanism description for the single resolver.
Record counts are presented without verification date or derivation method, so they may be stale.
The term edge-source is introduced as important but not defined in this file.
The field-index triple-store is noted as untraversed without explaining why or what would enable traversal.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and a markdown body.
Uses board:// addresses and item ids for identity and linking.
References sibling items via short codes like F2 and F6.
Represents file paths and terms with inline code spans.
Uses bold labels and numbered lists for structured exposition.
notes: A design ledger entry that maps eight graph layers onto a shared resolver/registry and flags edge-source convergence as the next open problem.
questions: What exactly does edge-source mean, and what is item F6?
Which file or component implements the single node resolver?
How does the resolver handle node identity across execution, corpus, board, type, navigation, code, extraction, and field-index layers?
What is the relationship between the eight layers and the single edge registry in practice?
Why is the field-index triple-store untraversed, and what would make it traversable?
Are the 18.8k and 11.5k record counts current as of the file creation date?
What do run:// addresses and near/far space set-difference specifically denote?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document graph layers and their convergence status, which matches what the file actually does.
apparent_themes: Architectural documentation in a ledger/noticeboard format
Separation of node identity, edge vocabulary, and edge source concerns
intention_signals: The file seems to be making a design argument that the system is closer to a unified graph architecture than it appears, because node resolution and edge vocabulary are already shared, and it wants to focus attention on the still-divergent edge-source problem.
novelty: It treats eight otherwise separate subgraphs—spanning runtime execution, semantic corpus relations, board links, type wiring, navigation, code dependencies, extraction embeddings, and field triples—as one converging graph stack with a common resolver and registry.
contribution: A neutral inventory of graph layers and their file locations, plus the assertion that node resolution and edge vocabulary are already shared, which a fused system would keep as a reference point for what is already unified and what still needs alignment.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item cataloging eight graph layers across execution, corpus, board, type, navigation, code, extraction, and field-index domains, noting shared node resolution and edge-registry mechanisms while flagging edge-source convergence as unresolved.
intention_for_embedding: To record and communicate the current convergence state of the system's graph architecture and direct attention to the remaining edge-source unification problem.
